definition: To run very fast for a short time.
example: Athletes sprint toward the finish line.
etymology: From Scandinavian roots meaning to jump.

definition: A metal object that pulls iron toward itself.
example: The magnet picked up paper clips.

definition: Steps that tell you how to cook something.
example: We followed the recipe for muffins.

definition: Keys you press to type letters on a screen or computer.
example: She practiced typing on the keyboard.

definition: A large group of animals that stay together.
example: A herd of deer crossed the field.

definition: A playful mammal that swims well and eats fish.
example: The otter floated on its back while cracking a shell.

definition: A flat breakfast cake cooked on a griddle.
example: We flipped pancakes on Saturday morning.

definition: A gym game where you throw soft balls and dodge throws.
example: His team won dodgeball by catching the last throw.

definition: The blinking line or arrow that shows where you type.
example: She moved the cursor to fix the typo.

definition: A mountain that can melt rock and shoot ash into the air.
example: Scientists studied gases above the quiet volcano.

definition: A salty legume snack; check allergy rules before sharing.
example: Trail mix included raisins and peanuts.

definition: A race where each runner covers part of the distance.
example: They practiced baton passes for the relay.

definition: A smart ocean mammal that swims in pods.
example: The dolphin leaped beside the tour boat.
